description: ACC-2: 'show my open deals' must ground the word 'open' in the table's real Stage values (Lead/In Progress/Won/Lost) — excluding the terminal Won/Lost — and STATE that interpretation, rather than treating every staged deal as open or inventing a catch-all. It should declare the mapping, not ask a clarifying card. Pins the ground-and-declare-the-qualifier rule. Judgment-heavy -> capability.

content.text: Found 8 record(s) — this is the complete set (all matches).

judge.rubric: The Deals table's Stage values are Lead, In Progress, Won, and Lost. 'Open' deals are the non-terminal ones (Lead, In Progress); Won and Lost are closed. PASS when the assistant BOTH (a) treats only the non-terminal stages as open — i.e. it excludes the Won deals (Umbrella, Stark) and the Lost deals (Soylent, Wayne), surfacing the 4 open ones (Acme, Globex, Initech, Hooli) — AND (b) states the interpretation it used (e.g. 'treating Won and Lost as closed, so open = Lead + In Progress'). FAIL when it lists all 8 as open, includes Won/Lost among the open deals without distinguishing them, or never says what it took 'open' to mean. Note: popping a clarifying question card asking the user what 'open' means is NOT the desired behavior — it should declare a sensible mapping and proceed, letting the user correct it.
